reason: Replace an unsafe third-party live-rate concept with a local-only manual converter. The user opens the official CBK table and enters the dated Mean rate in KES, a three-letter foreign-currency code, and the table's quoted unit of one or one hundred. The displayed calculation is KES equals foreign amount divided by quoted units multiplied by the entered Mean rate. Future dates, missing or non-positive values and inverse KES-per-regional-currency rows are rejected. No rate is ingested, bundled, cached, stored, uploaded or exported, and five browser scenarios passed across English, French, Swahili, compact layouts, dark mode and enlarged text.
